sections.biography-source-undefined: Zhang Shao was the second son of Zhang Fei and inherited his noble rank. He served as Palace Attendant and Vice Secretariat Minister in the Shu administration. In 263, the Emperor Liu Shan surrendered to Wei after Wei launched a military campaign against Shu. full_text:
Zhang Shao 張紹
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Zhang Shao (張紹)was born in Zhuo commandery. Surrendered to Wei upon its conquest of Shu. Zhang Shao was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom and the Wei Kingdom.

BiographySource Undefined
Zhang Shao was the second son of Zhang Fei and inherited his noble rank. He served as Palace Attendant and Vice Secretariat Minister in the Shu administration. In 263, the Emperor Liu Shan surrendered to Wei after Wei launched a military campaign against Shu. He sent Zhang Shao, along with Deng Liang, to present the seal and ribbon to the Wei general Deng Ai. He was enfeoffed by Wei as a ranged marquis. Like the rest of the Shu officials, he presumably went to Luoyang.
